title: Product Review
use_when: Use Product Review when a product plan, PRD, feature scope, acceptance criteria, or learning loop needs product judgment before design, engineering, QA, release, or growth work proceeds.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Product Review for market-thesis challenge, visual design critique, architecture review, QA execution, release mechanics, documentation sync, DX audit, or external comms review; route to those gates directly.
output_artifact: Product review with product judgment, requirement risks, priority call, decision, and handoff
framework_fit: Product Review fits the product-management gate inside 0-1, operate, and grow: it checks that scope, user value, requirements, priorities, metrics, and acceptance criteria are coherent before specialist reviewers or builders proceed.
failure_modes: Acts like a strategy review and reopens the market thesis instead of judging whether the product scope and requirements are ready for the next gate.
Approves a plan without testable acceptance criteria, explicit non-goals, metric intent, and a clear split between must-have and optional work.
Duplicates design, engineering, QA, release, docs, DX, or comms review instead of calling those gates when the issue belongs there.

examples.prompt: Use $product-review to check this PRD scope, requirements, acceptance criteria, metrics, and next gate before engineering starts.
examples.expected_artifact: Product review with product judgment, requirement risks, priority call, decision, and handoff
